intro: Leibnitzia kunzeana, also known as Kunze's or Leibnitzia, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and Tibet, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Leibnitzia kunzeana include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal supplements and capsules.
